name: Generalized Linear Models (Nelder & Wedderburn, 1972)
description: The original 1972 JRSS paper by Nelder and Wedderburn that introduced the unified framework of Generalized Linear Models. Foundation for all GLM work in actuarial science.

name: Generalized Linear Models, 2nd Edition (McCullagh & Nelder, 1989)
description: The definitive textbook on GLMs by McCullagh and Nelder. Covers exponential family distributions, link functions, estimation, and diagnostics. The standard reference for GLM theory.

name: Non-Life Insurance Pricing with Generalized Linear Models (Ohlsson & Johansson, 2010)
description: A Springer EAA textbook specifically focused on applying GLMs to non-life insurance pricing. Covers multiplicative tariff models, model selection, and practical implementation.

name: Generalized Linear Models for Insurance Data (de Jong & Heller, 2008)
description: Cambridge University Press textbook covering GLMs tailored for insurance applications. Includes claim frequency, severity modeling, Tweedie distributions, and practical examples.

name: Computational Actuarial Science with R (Charpentier, 2014)
description: Edited volume covering computational methods in actuarial science with R, including extensive chapters on GLMs for insurance pricing and reserving.

name: Statistical Methods for Insurance Pricing — Lecture Notes (Mario V. Wuthrich & Michael Merz)
description: Comprehensive SSRN manuscript on statistical methods for insurance pricing including GLMs, GAMs, and machine learning approaches. Free to download.

name: Exponential Dispersion Models (Jorgensen, 1987)
description: Jorgensen's seminal paper on exponential dispersion models which provides the theoretical foundation for Tweedie distributions used in insurance GLMs.

name: Fitting Tweedie's Compound Poisson Model to Insurance Claims Data (Smyth & Jorgensen, 2002)
description: Key paper in Scandinavian Actuarial Journal on fitting Tweedie compound Poisson models to insurance claims data, demonstrating practical application of the Tweedie GLM.

name: Double Generalized Linear Models (Smyth, 1989)
description: Paper introducing double GLMs where both the mean and the dispersion are modeled, useful in insurance for heteroscedastic claims data.

name: Predictive Modeling Applications in Actuarial Science, Vol 1: Predictive Modeling Techniques (Frees, Derrig & Meyers, 2014)
description: Cambridge University Press volume covering predictive modeling techniques including GLMs applied to insurance data. Covers frequency-severity models and pure premium approaches.

name: Regression Modeling with Actuarial and Financial Applications (Frees, 2010)
description: Textbook by Edward Frees covering regression and GLM methods with actuarial and financial applications. Available with online supplements.

name: An Introduction to Statistical Learning (James, Witten, Hastie, Tibshirani)
description: Free online textbook (ISLR) with comprehensive coverage of GLMs. While not actuarial-specific, it provides essential statistical learning foundations including logistic regression and Poisson regression.

name: The Elements of Statistical Learning (Hastie, Tibshirani, Friedman)
description: Free online textbook (ESL) providing advanced coverage of statistical learning methods including GLMs, GAMs, and regularization techniques applicable to insurance modeling.

name: Generalized Linear Models with Examples in R (Dunn & Smyth, 2018)
description: Springer textbook covering GLM theory and practice with R examples. Includes Tweedie distributions and applications relevant to actuarial modeling.

name: Stochastic Loss Reserving Using Generalized Linear Models (Taylor & McGuire, 2016)
description: CAS monograph on using GLMs for stochastic loss reserving. Covers chain-ladder as a GLM, over-dispersed Poisson models, and bootstrap methods.

name: A Practitioner's Introduction to Stochastic Reserving (England & Verrall, 2002)
description: British Actuarial Journal paper showing how the chain-ladder technique can be formulated as a GLM, providing a bridge between traditional and statistical reserving methods.

name: Stochastic Claims Reserving Methods in Insurance (England & Verrall, 2002)
description: ASTIN Bulletin paper providing a comprehensive survey of stochastic claims reserving methods, with emphasis on GLM-based approaches.

name: Pricing in General Insurance (Taylor, 1986 / updated editions)
description: Classic actuarial textbook on general insurance pricing. Covers the theory and practice of premium rating including GLM applications.

name: Modern Actuarial Risk Theory Using R (Kaas, Goovaerts, Dhaene, Denuit, 2008)
description: Springer textbook covering actuarial risk theory with R implementations, including chapters on GLMs for premium calculation and credibility theory.

name: Tweedie Distributions (Tweedie, 1984)
description: Original paper by M.C.K. Tweedie introducing the family of distributions that bears his name. Fundamental to compound Poisson-Gamma models in insurance pricing.

name: From GLMs to Machine Learning for Insurance Pricing (Wuthrich, 2019)
description: arXiv paper by Mario Wuthrich bridging traditional actuarial GLMs and modern machine learning methods for insurance pricing. Compares GLMs with neural networks.

name: Case Studies in Actuarial GLMs - Frequency-Severity Models (Wuthrich, 2022)
description: arXiv paper presenting case studies of actuarial GLMs for frequency-severity models in motor insurance pricing.

name: Statistical Foundations of Actuarial Learning and its Applications (Wuthrich & Merz, 2023)
description: Comprehensive arXiv monograph covering statistical foundations of actuarial learning, from GLMs to deep learning. Includes chapters on Poisson, gamma, and Tweedie regression.

name: Bias Regularization in Neural Network Models for General Insurance Pricing (Wuthrich, 2020)
description: arXiv paper comparing GLM and neural network approaches for insurance pricing, with methods for combining GLM structure with neural network flexibility.

name: Boosting Poisson Regression Models for Insurance Claims Frequency (Denuit & Lang, 2004)
description: ASTIN Bulletin paper on boosting methods for Poisson regression in insurance claim frequency modeling, extending the GLM framework.

name: Credibility Theory and Generalized Linear Models (Nelder & Verrall, 1997)
description: ASTIN Bulletin paper connecting Buhlmann credibility theory with GLMs, showing that credibility estimators can be viewed as random effects in a GLM framework.

name: Effective Statistical Learning Methods for Actuaries I: GLMs and Extensions (Denuit, Hainaut, Trufin, 2019)
description: First volume in a Springer Actuarial series covering GLMs and their extensions for actuarial applications. Includes Poisson, negative binomial, gamma, and Tweedie models.

name: Effective Statistical Learning Methods for Actuaries II: Tree-Based Methods and Extensions (Denuit, Hainaut, Trufin, 2019)
description: Second volume covering tree-based methods and their comparison with GLMs for actuarial modeling.

name: Effective Statistical Learning Methods for Actuaries III: Neural Networks and Extensions (Denuit, Hainaut, Trufin, 2020)
description: Third volume covering neural networks as extensions of GLMs for actuarial applications.

name: The Use of Generalized Linear Models in Actuarial Work (Haberman & Renshaw, 1996)
description: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society applied statistics paper surveying GLM applications in actuarial work including mortality, morbidity, and general insurance.

name: Loss Models: From Data to Decisions (Klugman, Panjer, Willmot)
description: Wiley textbook widely used in actuarial education (SOA syllabus). Covers loss distributions, frequency and severity models, and GLM applications.

name: CAS Monograph No. 5: Generalized Linear Models for Insurance Rating (Goldburd, Khare, Tevet, 2016)
description: Casualty Actuarial Society monograph providing a practical guide to GLMs for insurance rating. Covers model building, variable selection, and validation in insurance contexts.

name: Interpreting Generalized Linear Models for Insurance (Denuit et al., 2007)
description: Insurance: Mathematics and Economics paper on interpretation of GLM parameters in insurance pricing contexts.

name: Multivariate Frequency-Severity Regression Models in Insurance (Frees & Valdez, 2008)
description: Paper on multivariate frequency-severity regression models using GLMs for insurance ratemaking. Published in Insurance: Mathematics and Economics.

name: Hierarchical Insurance Claims Modeling (Frees, 2015)
description: JASA paper on hierarchical models extending GLMs for insurance claims with random effects and longitudinal structure.

name: SOA Study Note on Generalized Linear Models (SOA)
description: Society of Actuaries study note on GLMs for actuarial exam preparation. Covers core GLM concepts in an actuarial context.

name: Combining Machine Learning and GLMs for Insurance Pricing (Schelldorfer & Wuthrich, 2019)
description: Annals of Actuarial Science paper on combining traditional GLMs with neural networks for insurance pricing, the Combined Actuarial Neural Network (CANN) approach.

name: Actuarial Data Science Tutorials (Swiss Association of Actuaries)
description: SSRN collection of actuarial data science tutorials covering GLMs, tree-based models, and neural networks for insurance applications.

name: Individual Claims Reserving using Generalized Linear Models (Antonio & Plat, 2014)
description: Annals of Actuarial Science paper on using GLMs for individual claims reserving rather than aggregate triangle-based methods.

name: Poisson Regression (Cameron & Trivedi chapter on count data)
description: Regression Analysis of Count Data by Cameron and Trivedi. Core reference for Poisson and negative binomial regression used in insurance claim frequency modeling.

name: Insurance Risk Classification - Lecture Notes (ETH Zurich, Wuthrich)
description: ETH Zurich lecture notes by Mario Wuthrich on insurance risk classification using GLMs and machine learning methods.

name: Noll, Salzmann & Wuthrich - Insights from Inside Neural Networks (2020)
description: arXiv paper comparing GLMs with neural networks for insurance pricing using French motor third party liability data, providing insights on model interpretability.

name: Data Analytics for Non-Life Insurance Pricing (Wuthrich, 2020 lecture notes)
description: SSRN monograph providing comprehensive lecture notes on data analytics for non-life insurance pricing, covering GLMs, regularization, and machine learning.

name: GLMs for Insurance Ratemaking - CAS Educational Materials
description: CAS educational material on using GLMs for insurance ratemaking. Practical guide for practicing actuaries.

name: Lee and Lin - Delta Boosting for GLMs (2018)
description: arXiv paper on delta boosting implementation for GLMs in insurance pricing applications.

name: Tweedie's Compound Poisson Model with Concentrated Mass at Zero (Dunn & Smyth, 2005)
description: Paper on evaluation of Tweedie exponential dispersion model densities, important for implementing Tweedie GLMs in insurance contexts.

name: Mildenhall - A Systematic Relationship between Minimum Bias and Generalized Linear Models (1999)
description: CAS paper showing the relationship between traditional minimum bias procedures and GLMs in insurance classification ratemaking.

name: Anderson et al. - A Multivariate Exponential Distribution (Renshaw, 1994)
description: ASTIN Bulletin paper by Renshaw on modelling the claims process using GLMs, one of the early applications of GLMs to insurance claims.

name: Richman & Wuthrich - Neural Network Extensions of GLMs (2021)
description: arXiv paper proposing a framework that embeds GLMs within neural networks, allowing actuaries to leverage both traditional GLM structure and deep learning flexibility.

name: Henckaerts et al. - Boosting Insights in Insurance Tariff Plans with Tree-Based Machine Learning (2021)
description: arXiv paper comparing GLMs with gradient boosting machines for insurance tariff construction, using Belgian motor insurance data.

name: So et al. - Cost-sensitive Multi-class AdaBoost for Insurance Pricing (2021)
description: arXiv paper extending GLM-based insurance pricing with cost-sensitive boosting methods.

name: OpenActTextbooks - Loss Data Analytics (Free Online Textbook)
description: Free online open-source actuarial textbook covering loss data analytics including GLMs for frequency and severity modeling. Community-developed resource.

name: Nelder & Verrall - Credibility Theory and Generalized Linear Models (1997)
description: Key ASTIN Bulletin paper connecting Buhlmann-Straub credibility theory with the GLM framework, showing that credibility can be expressed as a mixed GLM.

name: Clark - GLMs in Practice: Questions and Answers (CAS, 2014)
description: CAS presentation by David Clark on practical questions and answers about using GLMs in insurance, aimed at practicing actuaries.
